medications.indication_rationale: Antiplatelet therapy reduces thrombotic risk in suspected unstable angina/ACS and is appropriate to advise now unless contraindicated. The source gave aspirin 100 mg stat.
medications.dose_route_frequency: aspirin 300 mg orally stat then 100 mg daily (source gave 100 mg stat) - unless allergic or actively bleeding

medications.indication_rationale: Short-acting sublingual GTN relieves anginal episodes by coronary/venous dilatation and is part of interim management, with clear instructions and safety-netting. The source prescribed GTN spray.
medications.dose_route_frequency: GTN spray sublingually during an episode; if pain persists beyond ~5 minutes repeat, and if not relieved after up to 3 doses over 15 minutes call 000
contraindications_in_this_case: Avoid if using PDE5 inhibitors (e.g. sildenafil); may cause hypotension/headache - sit down when using

medications.indication_rationale: Aggressive LDL lowering is essential in coronary disease; adherence should be corrected and dose optimised, generally via the assessing team/GP rather than blind over telehealth.
medications.dose_route_frequency: optimise statin therapy and adherence (dose set by the prescriber - not fabricated here)

integrative_alternative_therapies.therapy_name: Waiting for a routine GP appointment / self-managing with rest
integrative_alternative_therapies.rationale: New accelerating angina requires urgent same-day assessment; deferral risks progression to myocardial infarction.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Stop smoking immediately; when the urge to smoke arises, substitute a glass of water and five slow breaths.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Smoking is a potent coronary vasoconstrictor and atherogenic factor; cessation directly reduces anginal frequency and cardiovascular risk.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Avoid exertion until assessed, and rest if pain occurs; carry the GTN spray at all times.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Avoiding provocation reduces ischaemic episodes while awaiting urgent assessment.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Take the cholesterol tablet consistently and the aspirin as advised.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Consistent statin and antiplatelet therapy reduce cardiovascular events.

patient_education_points.point: Your chest tightness on exertion, spreading to your arm and jaw and easing with rest, is a classic sign of angina - narrowed heart arteries. Because it's new and getting worse, it needs urgent same-day heart assessment, not a wait-and-see.

Please attend urgent same-day cardiac assessment (emergency department or a rapid chest-pain clinic) for a heart tracing and blood tests. Take an aspirin now unless you're allergic or have a bleeding problem, and use the GTN spray for episodes.
Call 000 immediately if the chest pain comes on at rest, lasts more than 15 minutes, or is not relieved after using the GTN spray up to three times - that can mean a heart attack.
Stopping smoking and taking your cholesterol tablet regularly are the most important things you can do to reduce the chance of a heart attack.

follow_up_plan.interval: Urgent same-day cardiac assessment (ED/rapid-access chest-pain pathway); GP review within 24 hours for results
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: ECG/troponin results and ACS risk stratification, functional/anatomical coronary testing (stress echo/angiography), initiation/optimisation of antiplatelet, statin and anti-anginal therapy, smoking-cessation support, and cardiovascular risk management
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Chest pain at rest, lasting >15 minutes, or unrelieved by GTN (up to 3 doses) - call 000 immediately.

must_include_items: Recognition of new-onset/accelerating angina as possible unstable angina/ACS
Urgent same-day cardiac assessment (ECG/troponin/monitoring) - not routine referral
Interim aspirin (unless contraindicated) and GTN with instructions
Explicit ACS safety-netting (rest pain, >15 min, or unrelieved by GTN -> 000)
acceptable_alternatives: Emergency department or a rapid-access chest-pain clinic same day are both acceptable provided ECG/troponin and monitoring are secured
'Angina', 'narrowed heart arteries', 'unstable angina/possible ACS' are acceptable framings
errors_of_omission: Not recognising unstable angina / deferring to routine care
No aspirin or GTN advised (without contraindication)
errors_of_commission: Attributing the pain to indigestion or muscle strain
Advising the patient to wait for a routine appointment
Reassuring because he is pain-free at rest
Telling the patient a normal resting ECG would exclude a problem
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: HIGH-ACUITY CASE: A pass requires (1) recognising possible unstable angina/ACS and arranging urgent same-day cardiac assessment AND (2) interim aspirin/GTN with explicit ACS safety-netting. Attributing this to indigestion/strain or deferring to routine care is a dangerous under-triage risking myocardial infarction.
